Ran terminal command: $content = @'
"use client";

/**
 * DashShell — Light-mode dashboard layout shell.
 * Used by /admin and /parent route layouts.
 *
 * Structure:
 *   ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
 *   │ Sidebar (240px, soft light-blue)  │ Top bar (#fff)   │
 *   │  Logo                             │ Page title/user  │
 *   │  Nav groups (collapsible)         ├──────────────────│
 *   │   ▾ Section label                 │  Page content    │
 *   │     › Nav item                    │  bg: #f6f9fc     │
 *   │  Footer: ← Student View           │  overflow-y scroll│
 *   └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
 */

import Link from "next/link";
import { usePathname } from "next/navigation";
import { useMemo, useState, type ReactNode } from "react";
import LanguageSwitcher from "@/app/components/language-switcher";

// ── Types ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

export type DashNavItem = {
  href: string;
  label: string;
  /** Single emoji or 1–2 char text icon shown left of the label. */
  icon: string;
};

export type DashNavGroup = {
  /** Section header shown above the group in the sidebar. */
  label: string;
  items: DashNavItem[];
};

type DashShellProps = {
  children: ReactNode;
  navGroups: DashNavGroup[];
  /** e.g. "Admin Console" — shown in the top bar */
  productName: string;
  /** e.g. "Developer Tools" — shown under the logo in the sidebar */
  productSubtitle: string;
  /** User email or display name for the avatar / top-bar label */
  userLabel?: string;
};

// ── Sidebar nav link ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

function SidebarNavLink({ item }: { item: DashNavItem }) {
  const pathname = usePathname();
  const isActive =
    pathname === item.href || pathname.startsWith(item.href + "/");

  return (
    <Link
      href={item.href}
      className={[
        "group flex items-center gap-2.5 rounded-lg px-3 py-2 text-[13px] font-medium leading-snug transition-colors",
        isActive
          ? "bg-[#bddaf5] text-[#0e2d4e] shadow-sm"
          : "text-[#3a5a7c] hover:bg-[#cfe5f7] hover:text-[#0e2d4e]",
      ].join(" ")}
    >
      <span
        className="w-4 shrink-0 text-center text-[14px] leading-none"
        aria-hidden="true"
      >
        {item.icon}
      </span>
      <span className="truncate">{item.label}</span>
    </Link>
  );
}

// ── Sidebar group (collapsible) ────────────────────────────────────────────────

function SidebarNavGroup({ group }: { group: DashNavGroup }) {
  const pathname = usePathname();
  // Auto-open the group that contains the active page
  const hasActive = group.items.some(
    (item) => pathname === item.href || pathname.startsWith(item.href + "/"),
  );
  const [open, setOpen] = useState(hasActive);

  return (
    <div>
      <button
        type="button"
        onClick={() => setOpen((v) => !v)}
        className="flex w-full items-center justify-between px-3 py-1.5 rounded-md text-[11px] font-semibold uppercase tracking-widest text-[#6b8fae] hover:text-[#3a5a7c] hover:bg-[#d4e9f7] transition-colors"
        aria-expanded={open}
      >
        <span>{group.label}</span>
        <svg
          width="10"
          height="10"
          viewBox="0 0 10 10"
          fill="none"
          aria-hidden="true"
          className={`shrink-0 transition-transform duration-150 ${open ? "rotate-180" : "rotate-0"}`}
        >
          <path
            d="M1.5 3.5 5 7l3.5-3.5"
            stroke="currentColor"
            strokeWidth="1.5"
            strokeLinecap="round"
            strokeLinejoin="round"
          />
        </svg>
      </button>

      {open && (
        <div className="mt-0.5 space-y-0.5 pl-1 pb-1">
          {group.items.map((item) => (
            <SidebarNavLink key={item.href} item={item} />
          ))}
        </div>
      )}
    </div>
  );
}

// ── Sidebar content ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

function SidebarContent({
  navGroups,
  productSubtitle,
}: {
  navGroups: DashNavGroup[];
  productSubtitle: string;
}) {
  return (
    <div
      className="flex h-full flex-col safe-area-top safe-area-left safe-area-bottom"
      style={{ background: "#e4f0fa", borderRight: "1px solid #c1d9ee" }}
    >
      {/* ── Logo / branding ── */}
      <div
        className="flex h-14 shrink-0 items-center gap-3 px-4"
        style={{ borderBottom: "1px solid #c1d9ee" }}
      >
        <div className="flex h-8 w-8 shrink-0 items-center justify-center rounded-lg bg-[#635bff] text-[12px] font-bold text-white shadow-sm">
          K
        </div>
        <div className="min-w-0">
          <p className="truncate text-[13px] font-semibold leading-tight text-[#0e2d4e]">
            Koydo
          </p>
          <p className="truncate text-[11px] leading-tight text-[#6b8fae]">
            {productSubtitle}
          </p>
        </div>
      </div>

      {/* ── Navigation ── */}
      <nav
        className="flex-1 overflow-y-auto py-3 px-2 space-y-2"
        aria-label="Dashboard navigation"
      >
        {navGroups.map((group) => (
          <SidebarNavGroup key={group.label} group={group} />
        ))}
      </nav>

      {/* ── Footer ── */}
      <div
        className="shrink-0 px-4 py-3"
        style={{ borderTop: "1px solid #c1d9ee" }}
      >
        <Link
          href="/dashboard"
          className="flex items-center gap-2 text-[12px] text-[#6b8fae] transition-colors hover:text-[#3a5a7c]"
        >
          <svg
            width="12"
            height="12"
            viewBox="0 0 12 12"
            fill="none"
            aria-hidden="true"
          >
            <path
              d="M7.5 2L3.5 6l4 4"
              stroke="currentColor"
              strokeWidth="1.5"
              strokeLinecap="round"
              strokeLinejoin="round"
            />
          </svg>
          Student View
        </Link>
      </div>
    </div>
  );
}

// ── Shell ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

export default function DashShell({
  children,
  navGroups,
  productName,
  productSubtitle,
  userLabel,
}: DashShellProps) {
  const pathname = usePathname();
  const [sidebarOpen, setSidebarOpen] = useState(false);
  const avatarLetter = (userLabel ?? "A").charAt(0).toUpperCase();
  const activeNavItemLabel = useMemo(() => {
    const allItems = navGroups.flatMap((group) => group.items);
    const active = allItems.find(
      (item) => pathname === item.href || pathname.startsWith(`${item.href}/`),
    );
    return active?.label ?? productName;
  }, [navGroups, pathname, productName]);

  return (
    // Force light color scheme for the entire dashboard shell
    <div
      className="flex h-screen overflow-hidden bg-[#f6f9fc]"
      style={{ colorScheme: "light" }}
    >
      {/* ── Mobile backdrop ── */}
      {sidebarOpen && (
        <div
          className="fixed inset-0 z-20 bg-black/30 md:hidden"
          onClick={() => setSidebarOpen(false)}
          aria-hidden="true"
        />
      )}

      {/* ── Sidebar — fixed 240px on md+, off-canvas on mobile ── */}
      <aside
        className={[
          "fixed inset-y-0 left-0 z-30 w-56 transition-transform duration-200 ease-out md:w-60",
          sidebarOpen ? "translate-x-0" : "-translate-x-full md:translate-x-0",
        ].join(" ")}
        aria-label="Sidebar"
      >
        <SidebarContent
          navGroups={navGroups}
          productSubtitle={productSubtitle}
        />
      </aside>

      {/* ── Main column ── */}
      <div className="flex min-w-0 flex-1 flex-col md:pl-60">
        {/* ── Top bar ── */}
        <header
          className="safe-area-top sticky top-0 z-10 flex h-14 shrink-0 items-center justify-between px-4 md:px-6"
          style={{ borderBottom: "1px solid #dde6f0", background: "#ffffff" }}
        >
          <div className="flex min-w-0 items-center gap-3">
            {/* Mobile hamburger */}
            <button
              type="button"
              className="inline-flex h-8 w-8 items-center justify-center rounded-md text-[#6b8fae] hover:bg-[#e4f0fa] md:hidden transition-colors"
              onClick={() => setSidebarOpen(true)}
              aria-label="Open navigation"
            >
              <svg
                width="18"
                height="18"
                viewBox="0 0 18 18"
                fill="none"
                aria-hidden="true"
              >
                <path
                  d="M2.25 4.5h13.5M2.25 9h13.5M2.25 13.5h13.5"
                  stroke="currentColor"
                  strokeWidth="1.5"
                  strokeLinecap="round"
                />
              </svg>
            </button>

            <div className="min-w-0">
              <span className="hidden text-[14px] font-semibold text-[#0e2d4e] md:block">
                {productName}
              </span>
              <p className="truncate text-[12px] font-medium text-[#4a7a9e] md:text-[11px]">
                {activeNavItemLabel}
              </p>
            </div>
          </div>

          {/* Right: language switcher + user avatar */}
          <div className="flex items-center gap-3">
            <LanguageSwitcher compact />
            {userLabel && (
              <span className="hidden text-[12px] text-[#6b8fae] md:block truncate max-w-48">
                {userLabel}
              </span>
            )}
            <div
              className="grid h-7 w-7 shrink-0 place-items-center rounded-full bg-[#bddaf5] text-[11px] font-bold text-[#0e2d4e]"
              title={userLabel}
              aria-hidden="true"
            >
              {avatarLetter}
            </div>
          </div>
        </header>

        {/* ── Page content (scrollable) ── */}
        <div className="flex-1 overflow-y-auto">
          {children}
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>
  );
}

# 1. Replace the Section function + add InfoTip + CONSOLE_NAV constants
$oldSection = @'
function Section({
  title,
  children,
}: {
  title: string;
  children: React.ReactNode;
}) {
  return (
    <section className="ui-soft-card p-5">
      <h2 className="text-lg font-semibold">{title}</h2>
      <div className="mt-4">{children}</div>
    </section>
  );
}
'@

$newSection = @'
// ── Console section navigation definition ────────────────────────────────────

type ConsoleSectionDef = { id: string; title: string; description: string; tip?: string };
type ConsoleCategoryDef = { id: string; label: string; icon: string; sections: ConsoleSectionDef[] };

const CONSOLE_NAV: ConsoleCategoryDef[] = [
  {
    id: "system", label: "System Status", icon: "⬡",
    sections: [
      { id: "system-health", title: "System Health",
        description: "An all-in-one view of your platform health. Use this first whenever something seems broken — it combines environment, database, and payment checks in a single summary.",
        tip: "Green = everything working. Yellow = warnings that may limit some features. Red = a blocking problem that needs urgent attention. Click 'Refresh All' to update the data." },
      { id: "env-checks", title: "Environment Checks",
        description: "Verifies that all required configuration values (such as API keys and connection strings) are correctly set on your server. Missing settings quietly disable features.",
        tip: "'Warn' means the feature still runs in a reduced mode. 'Fail' means the feature is completely off until the setting is added. Your developer can add these in the .env file." },
      { id: "db-status", title: "Database Status",
        description: "Checks that your database has all the tables the platform needs. A missing table usually means a database update has not yet been applied.",
        tip: "If tables are missing, ask your developer to run 'npx supabase db push'. This applies any pending database structure updates." },
      { id: "stripe-webhooks", title: "Payment Webhooks",
        description: "Monitors whether payment notifications from Stripe are arriving and being processed. This ensures subscriptions, upgrades, and cancellations take effect promptly.",
        tip: "Failed events mean a billing action may not have been applied to the user's account. Use 'Mark Stale as Failed' to clean up stuck events, then replay from the Stripe Dashboard if needed." },
    ],
  },
  {
    id: "users", label: "User Management", icon: "◎",
    sections: [
      { id: "create-account", title: "Create Account",
        description: "Manually create a new user account — useful for setting up admin access, test accounts, or helping a parent who cannot complete registration on their own.",
        tip: "Only grant Admin access if this person truly needs to manage platform settings. Granting admin to the wrong person gives full access to this console." },
      { id: "approvals", title: "Approvals",
        description: "Some sensitive actions (like deleting a user or issuing a refund) require a pre-approved request first. Create and approve the request here, then use its ID when performing the action.",
        tip: "The approval ID links the action to the approval record. Always create and approve the request before attempting the sensitive action — otherwise the action will be blocked." },
      { id: "role-management", title: "Role Management",
        description: "Change what a user is allowed to do: grant or remove admin access and parent-portal access for any existing account.",
        tip: "Type UPDATE_ROLES exactly in the confirmation box to protect against accidental changes. Be careful not to remove your own admin access." },
      { id: "account-recovery", title: "Account Recovery",
        description: "Generate a secure password-reset link for a user who is locked out. You will see the full link in the result — copy it and send it to the user by email.",
        tip: "The link is single-use and expires quickly. If the user misses it, simply generate a new one here." },
      { id: "account-reset", title: "Account Reset / Deletion",
        description: "Reset a learner's progress data, or permanently delete an account. These actions cannot be undone — always double-check the user ID before proceeding.",
        tip: "Soft delete hides the account but keeps data for compliance. Hard delete permanently removes everything. For a GDPR erasure request, use hard delete alongside a DSAR request in the Compliance section." },
    ],
  },
  {
    id: "exam", label: "Exam Operations", icon: "✎",
    sections: [
      { id: "exam-maintenance", title: "Exam Maintenance",
        description: "Find and fix common errors in learners' exam records — for example, inconsistent scores or stale attempt data. A Dry Run shows what would change without applying anything.",
        tip: "Always run Dry Run first to review the impact. Only click 'Run Apply' when you are comfortable with the dry run results. The process may take several minutes on a large platform." },
    ],
  },
  {
    id: "finance", label: "Finance & Billing", icon: "$",
    sections: [
      { id: "refunds", title: "Refunds",
        description: "Issue a partial or full refund for a payment. You need the Stripe payment ID (starts with 'pi_') and an approved refund request ID.",
        tip: "Leave the amount blank for a full refund. Enter the amount in cents for a partial refund (e.g. 999 = $9.99). You must create and approve a refund request first." },
      { id: "promotions", title: "Promotions & Sales",
        description: "Create discount codes or limited-time sales events. These are applied at checkout to reduce the price learners pay.",
        tip: "A promo code is a reusable code learners enter at checkout. A sales event automatically applies a discount during a set date/time window — ideal for seasonal promotions." },
      { id: "pricing", title: "Base Product Pricing",
        description: "Create a new Stripe price for an existing product. Use this when you need to update the monthly subscription price for a plan.",
        tip: "Creating a new price does not affect existing subscribers — only new checkouts will use it. Tick 'Make this the default checkout price' so new signups use the updated price." },
      { id: "lang-price-map", title: "Language Price Map",
        description: "Maps each subscription plan to its Stripe Price ID. This controls which Stripe prices are used when learners subscribe to language learning plans.",
        tip: "Stripe Price IDs always start with 'price_'. Find them in your Stripe Dashboard under Products. After updating, verify by running a test checkout." },
      { id: "lang-runtime", title: "Language Runtime Config",
        description: "Controls live settings for the language feature — pricing phase, number of free practice attempts, and default regional pricing tier — without needing a code deployment.",
        tip: "Phase 1 is introductory/lower pricing. Phase 2 and 3 increase prices as the feature matures. The geo tier default sets the price region shown when a user's location cannot be detected." },
      { id: "lang-pricing-ladders", title: "Unlock Pricing Ladders",
        description: "Sets the one-time cost for learners to unlock individual exam levels (Beginner, Intermediate, Advanced) by geographic region. Prices are entered in cents.",
        tip: "The 5 tiers reflect global purchasing-power differences — Tier 1 is highest pricing (e.g. USA/EU) and Tier 5 is lowest. Enter prices in cents (e.g. 999 = $9.99)." },
      { id: "lang-reconciliation", title: "Unlock Reconciliation",
        description: "Checks for and repairs discrepancies between what learners paid for and what their accounts show as unlocked. Runs automatically each month but can be triggered manually here.",
        tip: "If a learner reports paying for an exam level but not having access, run this reconciliation. 'Drift' means records were out of sync. 'Auto-fixed' means they were corrected automatically." },
    ],
  },
  {
    id: "support-cat", label: "Support Queue", icon: "✉",
    sections: [
      { id: "support-queue", title: "Support Queue",
        description: "View and manage support tickets submitted by parents or learners. Update each ticket's status as you work through it.",
        tip: "Set tickets to 'In Progress' while investigating and 'Resolved' when the issue is fixed. Closed tickets are archived and no longer visible to the submitter." },
    ],
  },
];

function getSectionDef(id: string): ConsoleSectionDef | undefined {
  for (const cat of CONSOLE_NAV) {
    const found = cat.sections.find((s) => s.id === id);
    if (found) return found;
  }
  return undefined;
}

// ── Info tooltip ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

function InfoTip({ tip }: { tip: string }) {
  const [open, setOpen] = useState(false);
  return (
    <span className="relative ml-1.5 inline-block align-middle">
      <button
        type="button"
        onClick={() => setOpen((v) => !v)}
        className="inline-flex h-[18px] w-[18px] items-center justify-center rounded-full bg-blue-100 text-[10px] font-bold text-blue-700 hover:bg-blue-200 transition-colors"
        aria-label="More information"
      >
        ?
      </button>
      {open && (
        <>
          <div
            className="fixed inset-0 z-40"
            onClick={() => setOpen(false)}
            aria-hidden="true"
          />
          <div className="absolute left-0 top-6 z-50 w-72 rounded-xl border border-blue-200 bg-white p-3 text-xs leading-relaxed text-slate-600 shadow-xl">
            {tip}
            <button
              type="button"
              onClick={() => setOpen(false)}
              className="mt-2 block text-[10px] text-slate-400 hover:text-slate-600"
            >
              Close
            </button>
          </div>
        </>
      )}
    </span>
  );
}

// ── Console secondary nav ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────

function ConsoleNavCategory({
  category,
  selectedId,
  onSelect,
  badges,
}: {
  category: ConsoleCategoryDef;
  selectedId: string;
  onSelect: (id: string) => void;
  badges: Record<string, number>;
}) {
  const hasActive = category.sections.some((s) => s.id === selectedId);
  const [open, setOpen] = useState(hasActive);

  return (
    <div className="mb-1">
      <button
        type="button"
        onClick={() => setOpen((v) => !v)}
        className="flex w-full items-center justify-between rounded-md px-2.5 py-1.5 text-[11px] font-semibold uppercase tracking-wider text-slate-500 hover:bg-slate-100 hover:text-slate-700 transition-colors"
        aria-expanded={open}
      >
        <span className="flex items-center gap-1.5">
          <span aria-hidden="true">{category.icon}</span>
          {category.label}
        </span>
        <svg
          width="8"
          height="8"
          viewBox="0 0 8 8"
          fill="none"
          aria-hidden="true"
          className={`shrink-0 transition-transform duration-150 ${open ? "rotate-180" : ""}`}
        >
          <path d="M1 2.5 4 5.5 7 2.5" stroke="currentColor" strokeWidth="1.5" strokeLinecap="round" strokeLinejoin="round" />
        </svg>
      </button>

      {open && (
        <div className="mt-0.5 space-y-0.5 pl-1 pb-1">
          {category.sections.map((section) => {
            const isActive = section.id === selectedId;
            const badge = badges[section.id] ?? 0;
            return (
              <button
                key={section.id}
                type="button"
                onClick={() => onSelect(section.id)}
                className={`flex w-full items-center justify-between rounded-md px-3 py-1.5 text-left text-[12px] font-medium transition-colors ${
                  isActive
                    ? "bg-blue-100 text-blue-900"
                    : "text-slate-600 hover:bg-slate-100 hover:text-slate-800"
                }`}
              >
                <span className="truncate">{section.title}</span>
                {badge > 0 && (
                  <span className="ml-1.5 flex h-4 min-w-4 items-center justify-center rounded-full bg-amber-400 px-1 text-[9px] font-bold text-white">
                    {badge}
                  </span>
                )}
              </button>
            );
          })}
        </div>
      )}
    </div>
  );
}

// ── Section card ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

function Section({
  title,
  description,
  tip,
  children,
}: {
  title: string;
  description?: string;
  tip?: string;
  children: React.ReactNode;
}) {
  return (
    <section className="rounded-xl border border-slate-200 bg-white p-5 shadow-sm">
      <div className="mb-1 flex items-center">
        <h2 className="text-[15px] font-semibold text-slate-800">{title}</h2>
        {tip && <InfoTip tip={tip} />}
      </div>
      {description && (
        <p className="mb-4 text-sm leading-relaxed text-slate-500">{description}</p>
      )}
      <div>{children}</div>
    </section>
  );
}
'@

  @("Role Management", "role-management", "Change what a user is allowed to do: grant or remove admin access and parent-portal access for any existing account.", "Type UPDATE_ROLES exactly in the confirmation box to prevent accidental changes. Be careful not to remove your own admin access."),
  @("Account Recovery", "account-recovery", "Generate a secure password-reset link for a user who is locked out of their account. The link will appear in the result — copy and send it to the user by email.", "The link is single-use and expires quickly. If the user misses it, simply generate a new one here at any time."),
  @("Account Reset / Deletion", "account-reset", "Reset a learner's progress data, or permanently delete an account. These actions cannot be undone — always verify the correct user ID before proceeding.", "Soft delete hides the account but keeps data for compliance reporting. Hard delete permanently removes everything. For a GDPR erasure request, use hard delete alongside a DSAR record in Compliance."),
  @("Refunds", "refunds", "Issue a partial or full refund for a payment. You need the Stripe payment ID (starts with pi_) and a pre-approved refund request ID.", "Leave the amount blank for a full refund. Enter the amount in cents for a partial refund (999 = 9.99). You must create and approve a refund request before using this form."),
  @("Promotions and Sales", "promotions", "Create discount codes or limited-time sales events. Promo codes are entered by learners at checkout; sales events apply discounts automatically within a date range.", "A promo code is reusable and can be shared publicly. A sales event applies automatically during a set time window — ideal for seasonal promotions."),
  @("Pricing", "pricing", "Create a new Stripe price for an existing product. Use this when you need to update the subscription price for a plan.", "Creating a new price does not change existing subscribers — only new checkouts will use it. Tick Make this the default checkout price so all new sign-ups use the updated amount."),
  @("Language Price Map", "lang-price-map", "Maps each subscription plan to its Stripe Price ID. This controls which Stripe prices are charged when learners subscribe to language learning plans.", "Stripe Price IDs always start with price_. Find them in your Stripe Dashboard under Products. After updating, verify by running a test checkout session."),
  @("Language Runtime Config", "lang-runtime", "Controls live settings for the language feature — pricing phase, free practice attempts, and default regional pricing tier — without requiring a code deployment.", "Phase 1 is introductory pricing. Phase 2 and 3 progressively increase prices as the feature matures. The geo tier default sets the pricing region shown when a user location cannot be detected."),
  @("Language Unlock Pricing Ladders", "lang-pricing-ladders", "Sets the one-time cost to unlock individual exam levels (Beginner, Intermediate, Advanced) by geographic region. All prices are entered in cents.", "The 5 tiers reflect global purchasing-power differences — Tier 1 is highest (e.g. USA/EU) and Tier 5 is lowest. Enter prices in cents (999 = 9.99)."),
  @("Language Unlock Reconciliation", "lang-reconciliation", "Checks for and repairs discrepancies between what learners paid for and what their accounts show as unlocked. Runs automatically monthly but can be triggered manually.", "If a learner reports paying for an exam level but not having access, run this reconciliation. Drift means records were out of sync. Auto-fixed means they were corrected automatically."),
  @("Support Queue", "support-queue", "View and manage support tickets submitted by parents or learners. Update each ticket status as you work through the requests.", "Set tickets to In Progress while investigating and Resolved once the issue is fixed. Closed tickets are archived and no longer shown to the original submitter.")
)

foreach ($t in $transitions) {
  $title = $t[0]
  $id = $t[1]
  $desc = $t[2]
  $tip = $t[3]
  $old = "      </Section>`n`n      <Section title=`"$title`">"
  $new = "      </Section>`n      )}`n`n      {selectedSectionId === `"$id`" && (<Section title=`"$title`" description=`"$desc`" tip=`"$tip`">"
  $c = $c.Replace($old, $new)
}

# Fix the final closing - change </Section>↵    </div>↵  );↵}
$c = $c.Replace("      </Section>`n    </div>`n  );`n}", "      </Section>`n      )}`n        </div>`n      </div>`n    </div>`n  );`n}")

[System.IO.File]::WriteAllText($file, $c, [System.Text.Encoding]::UTF8)
Write-Output "Done. Lines: $((Get-Content $file).Count). Contains selectedSectionId: $($c.Contains('selectedSectionId === `"env-checks`"'))"

# Check file ends correctly

Write-Output "--- Last 8 lines ---"
$lastLines | ForEach-Object { Write-Output $_ }

Completed: *TS check + commit + push* (8/8)

All done. Committed `b41f2e4` and pushed. Here's everything that shipped:

**Light mode & sidebar**
- dash-shell.tsx — `#e4f0fa` light blue sidebar, forced `colorScheme: light`, collapsible `SidebarNavGroup` components that auto-open for the active route

**Operations console split-panel**
- operations-console.tsx — left `<aside>` nav with 5 collapsible categories (System Status, User Management, Exam Operations, Finance & Billing, Support Queue) containing 18 sections; clicking a row renders only that section in the centre panel
- Each section has a plain-English `description` subtitle and an `InfoTip` `?` button with detailed context

**Error/status fixes**
- Exam maintenance banner is now **green** when `openExamMaintenanceAlerts === 0 && unacknowledgedRunSummaries === 0`, amber otherwise
- db-readiness.ts — `generated_module_sessions` added to the 37-table required list (fixes "1 missing" false alarm)

**Input height**
- globals.css — `min-height: 32px` baseline for all `input`, `select`, `textarea` elements (~5px gain on small `py-1` fields)

**Parent support tickets**
- parent/layout.tsx — Support added to Account nav group
- parent/support/page.tsx — server component fetches user's own tickets
- parent/support/support-client.tsx — submission form (subject, details, priority) + ticket history list, calls existing `/api/support/tickets`

**i18n Polish + Simplified Mandarin**
- translations.ts — `pl` added to `SUPPORTED_LOCALES`; `ACTIVE_LOCALES` expanded to `["en", "es", "zh", "pl"]`; `localeLabels` updated to `Polski` / `中文（简体）`; comprehensive Polish dictionary added for all nav, auth, dashboard, billing, and parent portal keys

---

## Strategic Alignment Report — Koydo vs. Consensus Research Findings

**Scope:** Non-advertising recommendations only. No changes have been made. This is a planning document.

---

### Executive Summary

The Consensus Research Report identifies five non-advertising pillars that drive long-term valuation, legal safety, and subscriber conversion in children's EdTech: (1) a rigorously enforced freemium feature gate, (2) a safe and premium-gated AI companion, (3) April 2026 COPPA rule compliance, (4) high-trust parent dashboard features, and (5) privacy certification (kidSAFE + Common Sense Privacy Seal).

The codebase audit shows Koydo has strong foundations in parental consent (VPC), age-gating, entitlement checks, and language-learning feature gating. However, **14 material gaps** exist. Eight are compliance-critical with the April 22, 2026 COPPA deadline less than two months away.

---

### PILLAR 1 — Freemium Feature Gate

**What the report recommends:** Hard daily activity limit for free users (ABCmouse model: 10 activities/day); gate multi-learner profiles, detailed reporting, offline mode, and AI features behind premium subscription. Make the free tier a zero-CAC conversion funnel, not a full product.

**Current state:**

| Area | Status |
|---|---|
| Content gating in explore grid (lock UI on premium modules) | ✅ Implemented |
| Language learning usage caps (scored attempts, pronunciation) | ✅ Implemented |
| Multi-learner profile cap for free tier | ❌ No limit — any user can create unlimited learner profiles |
| Direct URL access to premium lesson content | ❌ Lock UI is in the grid; lesson pages at `/lessons/[lessonId]` are not server-side gated — a free user with the URL can access premium content |
| Daily activity limit (any kind) | ❌ No daily cap exists for any tier |
| Offline mode gating | ❌ Offline progress DB (`src/lib/offline/progress-db.ts`) exists but no PWA service worker is wired and no subscription gate is present |
| Detailed reporting gated for parents | ❌ Parent reports page is fully accessible regardless of subscription tier |
| Upgrade nudge in active lesson / dashboard | ❌ No in-flow upgrade prompts exist |
| Free-vs-premium comparison / pricing page | ❌ No standalone pricing page exists in the app |

**Planned actions:**

1. **Server-side lesson gate** — Add subscription-tier check in [src/app/lessons/\[lessonId\]/page.tsx](src/app/lessons/[lessonId]/page.tsx) so premium lessons redirect to an upgrade page for free-tier users regardless of how they arrive.
2. **Multi-learner cap** — Enforce a configurable free-tier learner limit (e.g., 1 profile) in the onboarding flow at src/app/student/onboarding/ and in the parent dashboard "Add Learner" button.
3. **Daily activity cap** — Introduce a `daily_activity_count` tracking mechanism (Redis or Supabase row with date-keyed counter) and surface a soft paywall when the free daily limit is reached during a lesson session.
4. **Offline mode gate** — When PWA/service-worker work is started, wrap the offline sync toggle in a subscription entitlement check.
5. **Parent reporting gate** — Add a tier check at the top of src/app/parent/reports/ so free-tier parents see a preview with an upgrade prompt.
6. **In-flow upgrade nudges** — Add a non-intrusive upgrade banner at lesson completion for free-tier users (lesson end screen is the natural conversion moment).
7. **Pricing comparison page** — Create a `/pricing` page showing the free vs. premium feature comparison matrix modelled on ABCmouse Basic/Premium.

---

### PILLAR 2 — AI Companion Safety & Premium Gating

**What the report recommends:** LLM conversations must have a multi-layer safety system (system prompt persona lock, mid-call regex/classifier, programmatic hang-up <200ms), full transcript logging, parent-visible transcript dashboard, and email alerts on safety flags. Unlimited AI interactions gate behind premium tier. (Khanmigo / Duolingo Max model.)

**Current state:**

| Area | Status |
|---|---|
| AI companion endpoint (`/api/companion/chat`) | ✅ Exists |
| AI tutor endpoint (`/api/ai/tutor`) | ✅ Exists |
| Age-tier safety config with per-tier controls | ✅ Exists in src/lib/safety/age-tier.ts |
| Content safety classifier / moderation API on companion responses | ❌ Not implemented — no blocklist, no classifier, no moderation API call |
| Programmatic hang-up / stream abort on triggered safety classifier | ❌ Not implemented |
| Companion conversation logging to `ai_tutor_conversations` table | ❌ Companion chat is not logged (tutor endpoint may be — requires confirmation) |
| AI transcripts visible in parent portal | ❌ Parent portal shows no AI interaction history |
| Parent email alert on safety classifier flag | ❌ No email alert system for safety events |
| Subscription gate on AI companion (free tier cap or full gate) | ❌ AI follow-up (src/lib/ai/follow-up.ts), remediation worksheets (src/lib/ai/remediation-worksheet.ts), and parent AI interventions (src/app/api/parent/ai-interventions/route.ts) are ungated |

**Planned actions:**

1. **Safety moderation layer** — Add an OpenAI Moderation API (or equivalent) call before streaming each AI response in both `/api/companion/chat` and `/api/ai/tutor`. If the moderation score exceeds a threshold, abort the stream and return a safe fallback response.
2. **Programmatic hang-up** — Implement stream abort on safety flag using the AbortController pattern on the streaming response, targeting <200ms detection.
3. **Conversation logging** — Write every companion and tutor exchange (user message + assistant response + safety score) to `ai_tutor_conversations` with user ID, learner ID, and timestamp.
4. **Parent transcript view** — Add an "AI Conversations" section to the parent portal showing paginated conversation history per learner, with safety flags highlighted.
5. **Safety alert email** — When the moderation layer flags a message, dispatch an email to the parent's registered address via the existing email infrastructure.
6. **Subscription gating on AI features** — Wrap AI follow-up, remediation worksheets, and AI interventions in entitlement checks. Free-tier users get a defined monthly cap (e.g., 5 AI tutor interactions); premium gets unlimited.

---

### PILLAR 3 — COPPA 2026 Compliance (April 22, 2026 deadline — ~7 weeks away)

**What the report requires:** Data retention limits with automated enforcement, mandatory Written Information Security Program (WISP), separate granular parental consent for non-essential features (not bundled with account creation), dedicated Children's Privacy Notice, and a Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA).

**Current state:**

| Area | Status |
|---|---|
| Age gate with under-13 detection | ✅ Implemented (src/app/api/compliance/age-gate/route.ts) |
| VPC (parental consent) full flow with HMAC tokens | ✅ Implemented (src/lib/compliance/parent-consent-token.ts) |
| Policy versioning + per-user acceptance tracking | ✅ Implemented |
| Tracking consent with default-deny | ✅ Implemented (src/lib/consent/tracking-consent.ts) |
| DSAR intake API + admin console | ✅ Implemented |
| Cookie/analytics consent **distinguishing child profiles from adult accounts** | ❌ Current consent logic does not switch to a stricter mode when a child profile is the active learner |
| Automated data retention enforcement (scheduled deletion) | ❌ No cron job or Supabase scheduled function purges stale children's data per COPPA retention limits |
| Children's Privacy Notice (dedicated COPPA-specific disclosure page) | ❌ Privacy policy exists but no dedicated children's notice page |
| Written Information Security Program (WISP) | ❌ Not documented or referenced in code; required by the 2026 amendments |
| Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA) | ❌ `docs/` contains only a Google Play data safety questionnaire |
| DSAR user-facing submission UI | ❌ API exists but no parent-portal UI for parents to submit or track DSAR status |
| Separate consent toggle for non-essential data uses (distinct from account creation) | ❌ Parental consent is currently a single acceptance flow; the 2026 amendment requires granular, separate opt-ins |

**Planned actions:**

1. **Child-profile tracking lockdown** — Modify src/lib/consent/tracking-consent.ts to force `analytics = false` and `marketing = false` whenever an active learner profile is in session (child-context mode), irrespective of any adult account settings.
2. **Data retention cron job** — Create a Supabase scheduled function (or edge cron) that deletes or anonymises records for inactive child profiles older than the configured retention window (per COPPA 2026: no indefinite retention).
3. **Children's Privacy Notice page** — Add a dedicated `/legal/children-privacy` page with COPPA-specific disclosures (categories of data collected about children, purpose, third-party sharing) separate from the main privacy policy.
4. **Granular parental consent UI** — Add separate toggle sections inside the parent portal compliance page for: (a) AI-generated personalisation, (b) analytics/usage data, (c) communications, each with plain-language descriptions — not bundled with account acceptance.
5. **WISP document** — Create a written information security program covering access controls, breach response, and vendor management. Reference it from the admin compliance page.
6. **DPIA** — Document a Data Protection Impact Assessment covering all child-data processing flows, third-party integrations, and risk mitigations. Store in `docs/dpia.md` and reference from the compliance admin page.
7. **DSAR parent UI** — Add a "Data Rights" section to src/app/parent/compliance/ allowing parents to submit access requests, deletion requests, and track status without contacting support directly.

---

### PILLAR 4 — Parent Dashboard Trust & Transparency

**What the report recommends:** PIN protection on the parent portal, full encrypted AI transcript visibility, email alerts on safety flags, and comprehensive progress reporting for all curriculum areas — not just language learning.

**Current state:**

| Area | Status |
|---|---|
| Authentication into parent portal | ✅ Standard Supabase auth |
| Parent reporting: language learning progress | ✅ Implemented |
| Parent reporting: core curriculum progress (lessons, mastery, time on task) | ❌ Reports only cover language; overall curriculum progress not shown to parents |
| PIN or cognitive gate on parent portal | ❌ Not implemented — anyone logged into the parent account has full access |
| AI transcript visibility in parent portal | ❌ Not implemented |
| Email alerts for safety flags or unusual learner activity | ❌ Not implemented |

**Planned actions:**

1. **Curriculum progress reports** — Extend src/app/parent/reports/ to surface core curriculum metrics per learner: lessons completed, mastery scores, subjects covered, and time spent — using data already in `user_learning_progress` and `user_skill_mastery`.
2. **AI transcript section** — Add a "Learning Conversations" tab to the parent portal (dependent on Pillar 2, action 4) once logging is implemented.
3. **Safety email alerts** — Wire parent email notifications for safety-flagged AI events (dependent on Pillar 2, action 5) and optionally for when a learner hits the daily activity cap.
4. **PIN/cognitive gate** — Add an optional parent-portal PIN mechanism (4-digit numeric or arithmetic challenge per Apple/Google kids app guidelines) configurable in account settings, to prevent a child from accessing the parent portal on a shared device.

---

### PILLAR 5 — Privacy Certification Readiness

**What the report recommends:** Pursue kidSAFE Edu + AI Developer Seals (legal FTC Safe Harbor shield, 4–12 weeks, $6,500–$20,000) and the Common Sense Privacy Seal (a 200-point rubric, not a legal Safe Harbor but the gold standard for school and parent trust). Begin immediately given the April 2026 COPPA deadline.

**Current state:**

| Area | Status |
|---|---|
| kidSAFE or equivalent seal application initiated | ❌ Not referenced anywhere in the codebase or docs |
| Common Sense Privacy Seal assessment initiated | ❌ Not referenced |
| COPPA Safe Harbor program membership | ❌ Not in place |
| Privacy policy and COPPA disclosures sufficient for audit | ⚠️ Partial — policy exists but Children's Privacy Notice is missing (see Pillar 3) |

**Planned actions:**

1. **kidSAFE application preparation** — The codebase already has strong VPC, age-gating, and consent infrastructure. The main prerequisites to complete before applying are: Children's Privacy Notice page, data retention enforcement, WISP, granular consent UI, and AI safety moderation (covered in Pillars 2–3 above). Initiate application once those are in place; target audit window of 4–8 weeks.
2. **Common Sense Privacy rubric self-assessment** — Run an internal assessment against the 200-point Common Sense rubric. The rubric examines data collection minimisation, third-party sharing, and security practices. Expected score based on current state: ~60–70%; target 85%+.
3. **Document trust markers in-product** — Once certifications are obtained, add kidSAFE and Common Sense seal badges to the home page, pricing page, and parent portal to drive subscription conversion.

---

### PILLAR 6 — B2B / School Licensing Infrastructure

**What the report recommends:** Free educator tier (Epic! model) as a viral zero-cost distribution mechanism; home paywall when students try to access outside school hours/devices.

**Current state:**

| Area | Status |
|---|---|
| Organisation / school role infrastructure | ✅ Exists (src/lib/organizations/, src/lib/compliance/teacher-access.ts) |
| `language_school_annual` SKU | ⚠️ Defined with status `planned` in src/lib/language-learning/pricing-spec.ts — not live |
| Educator onboarding flow | ❌ No dedicated educator sign-up or classroom setup flow |
| B2B pricing or school district page | ❌ No public-facing educator or institutional pricing page |
| School-hours access gate (Epic! time gate model) | ❌ Not implemented |

**Planned actions:**

1. **Activate school SKU** — Move `language_school_annual` from `planned` to `active` in src/lib/language-learning/pricing-spec.ts and wire it to the Stripe/RevenueCat entitlement checks.
2. **Educator landing and onboarding** — Create a `/for-schools` landing page explaining the free educator tier and a simplified classroom setup flow (class code generation, bulk learner import).
3. **B2B contact / demo request** — Add a district/enterprise inquiry form for large-scale licensing conversations.
4. *(Lower priority)* **School-hours access gate** — When the educator tier is live, implement the Epic!-style home paywall (access outside assigned classroom hours requires a family subscription).

---

### Priority Order & Dependency Map

```
URGENT (before April 22, 2026)
├── Pillar 3A: Child-profile tracking lockdown
├── Pillar 3B: Data retention scheduled deletion
├── Pillar 3C: Children's Privacy Notice page
├── Pillar 3D: Granular parental consent UI
├── Pillar 2A/B: AI safety moderation + stream abort
├── Pillar 2C: Companion conversation logging
└── Pillar 5A: Begin kidSAFE application (requires above)

HIGH (Q2 2026)
├── Pillar 1A: Server-side lesson gate (URL bypass fix)
├── Pillar 1B: Multi-learner cap
├── Pillar 2D: Parent AI transcript view
├── Pillar 2E: Safety alert email
├── Pillar 4A: Curriculum progress reports for parents
├── Pillar 3E: DSAR parent UI
└── Pillar 3F: WISP + DPIA documents

MEDIUM (Q2–Q3 2026)
├── Pillar 1C: Daily activity cap
├── Pillar 1D: In-flow upgrade nudges
├── Pillar 1E: Pricing comparison page
├── Pillar 4D: PIN/cognitive gate on parent portal
├── Pillar 5B: Common Sense Privacy self-assessment
├── Pillar 6A: Activate school SKU
└── Pillar 6B: Educator landing page + onboarding

LOWER (Q3 2026)
├── Pillar 1F: Offline mode gate (when PWA is built)
├── Pillar 1G: Parent reporting gate (tier check)
├── Pillar 6C: District B2B contact page
└── Pillar 6D: School-hours access gate
```

---

### What Is Already Well-Aligned

The following areas match the report's recommendations and require no immediate action:

- **VPC (Verifiable Parental Consent)** — full HMAC-token email verification flow already exceeds minimum COPPA requirements
- **Age gate** — under-13 detection with consent redirect is in place
- **Language learning AI gating** — scored attempts, pronunciation, and translation are fully tier-gated
- **Tracking consent default-deny** — analytics are off by default
- **Policy acceptance versioning** — per-user, per-version acceptance records exist
- **DSAR admin workflow** — intake API and admin console visibility are working
- **Content lock UI** — premium modules are visually locked in the explore grid
- **DSA transparency notice** — present at `/legal/dsa`
- **Organisation/school role infrastructure** — code scaffolding exists even if educator flows are not yet public

I now have comprehensive research across all six pillars. Here is the full implementation plan:

---

## Plan: Strategic Alignment — Full Six-Pillar Implementation

**TL;DR:** 14 material gaps across 6 pillars, mapped to ~45 discrete code changes. The plan is phased by urgency: Phase 1 (COPPA-critical, before April 22), Phase 2 (safety + freemium integrity), Phase 3 (trust + B2B + certification). Each step references exact files, function signatures, and architectural patterns already in the codebase.

---

### Phase 1 — COPPA 2026 Compliance (Critical: before April 22, 2026)

**Step 1.1 — Child-profile tracking lockdown**
- Modify src/lib/consent/tracking-consent.ts to accept an `isChildSession: boolean` parameter
- When `isChildSession === true`, force `analytics: false` regardless of stored consent, and skip Mixpanel initialization entirely
- The caller (likely the root layout or a context provider) must pass the active learner's age-tier status from `student_profiles` or from `getAgeTierConfig()` in src/lib/safety/age-tier.ts
- Add a new utility: `isActiveUserChild(supabase, userId): Promise<boolean>` in src/lib/compliance/ that checks `user_profiles.birth_date` or the active student profile's age

**Step 1.2 — Children's Privacy Notice page**
- Create src/app/legal/children-privacy/page.tsx — a dedicated COPPA-specific notice covering: categories of child data collected, purposes, third-party recipients (Supabase, Stripe, OpenAI, ElevenLabs), parent rights (access, deletion, consent revocation), contact info
- Add this to src/lib/compliance/policies.ts as a new policy type `"children_privacy"` with version and effective date
- Link from the main privacy page and from the parent consent email template in src/lib/email/parent-consent-email.ts

**Step 1.3 — Granular parental consent UI**
- Modify the parent consent flow at src/app/api/compliance/parent-consent/route.ts to accept a `consentScopes` array (e.g., `["educational_data", "ai_personalization", "analytics_usage"]`)
- Store scopes in the `parent_consents.evidence` JSONB field alongside the existing token hash
- Create a new parent-facing consent management component in src/app/parent/compliance/ with separate toggles for each scope, a "Revoke All Consent" button, and inline explanations in plain language
- When consent is revoked, update `parent_consents.status` to `"denied"` and trigger data restriction (disable AI features, suppress analytics for that child)

**Step 1.4 — Consent revocation mechanism**
- Add a `POST /api/compliance/parent-consent/revoke` endpoint that:
  - Verifies the caller is the parent (via email match or authenticated parent user)
  - Sets `parent_consents.status = "denied"` and `user_profiles.parental_consent_status = "denied"`
  - Queues a DSAR erasure request automatically for the child's data
  - Returns confirmation with a 30-day grace period notice
- Wire this to a "Revoke Consent" button on the parent compliance page

**Step 1.5 — Parent-initiated DSAR flow**
- Add a "Data Rights" section to src/app/parent/compliance/page.tsx allowing parents to:
  - Submit access/erasure requests on behalf of their linked children
  - View request status and history
- Create `POST /api/privacy/dsar/parent` endpoint that accepts `childUserId` and validates parent linkage via `parent_consents` table
- Display submitted DSARs with status tracking in the parent compliance page

**Step 1.6 — Automated data retention enforcement**
- Create a new Supabase Edge Function or a cron-triggered API route (`/api/admin/cron/data-retention`) that runs daily:
  - Deletes `parent_consents` rows with `status = "pending_verification"` older than 7 days
  - Anonymises or deletes child data for accounts with `status = "denied"` after the 30-day grace period
  - Purges `ai_tutor_conversations` older than the configured retention window (e.g., 12 months for active users, 90 days for deleted accounts)
  - Purges `pronunciation_attempts` and `learning_events` older than the retention window
- Add a `data_retention_policy` config table or use `app_settings` to store retention windows
- Log all retention actions to `admin_action_logs`

**Step 1.7 — WISP and DPIA documents**
- Create docs/wisp.md — Written Information Security Program covering: access control policies, encryption standards (TLS 1.3, AES-256), breach response procedures, vendor management (Supabase, Stripe, OpenAI, ElevenLabs, Resend), employee training requirements
- Create docs/dpia.md — Data Protection Impact Assessment covering: all child-data processing flows, purpose limitation, data minimisation controls, risk matrix, mitigations
- Reference both from the admin compliance page at src/app/admin/compliance/ as downloadable links

---

### Phase 2 — AI Safety + Freemium Feature Gates (High Priority)

**Step 2.1 — AI safety moderation layer**
- Create src/lib/ai/moderation.ts with:
  - `moderateInput(text: string): Promise<ModerationResult>` — calls OpenAI Moderation API, returns `{ flagged: boolean, categories: string[], action: "allow" | "warn" | "block" }`
  - `moderateOutput(text: string): Promise<ModerationResult>` — same API for model responses
  - `buildSafetySystemPrompt(ageTier: AgeTier): string` — generates age-appropriate system prompt addendum with jailbreak resistance instructions
  - A regex blocklist for PII patterns (emails, phone numbers, addresses) and profanity
- Wire into src/app/api/companion/chat/route.ts:
  - Add auth check using `createSupabaseServerClient()` → `supabase.auth.getUser()`
  - Add IP rate limiting using existing `enforceIpRateLimit`
  - Call `moderateInput()` on user message before sending to OpenAI
  - Call `moderateOutput()` on the response before returning
  - If flagged, return a safe fallback: `"I can only help with learning topics! What subject would you like to explore?"`
  - Look up the active learner's age, call `getAgeTierConfig(age)`, enforce `allowFreeformChat` flag
- Wire into src/app/api/ai/tutor/route.ts:
  - Add `moderateInput()` before OpenAI call
  - Add `moderateOutput()` on response
  - If flagged, log the event with `{ flagged: true, categories }` in the `metadata` JSONB column of `ai_tutor_conversations`

**Step 2.2 — Companion conversation logging**
- Modify src/app/api/companion/chat/route.ts to write both user and assistant messages to `ai_tutor_conversations` with `source = "companion"` (add a new allowed value or use `metadata.channel = "companion"`)
- Include the moderation result in `metadata`

**Step 2.3 — Programmatic stream abort**
- For future streaming upgrades: implement `AbortController` with a 200ms timeout on the moderation check. If moderation flags content mid-stream, call `controller.abort()` and flush a safe fallback message
- For now (non-streaming endpoints): the moderation check happens before the response is returned, so the abort is simply returning the fallback response instead

**Step 2.4 — AI transcript visibility in parent portal**
- Create src/app/parent/ai-conversations/page.tsx (server component):
  - Auth + parent role check
  - Query `ai_tutor_conversations` joined with `student_profiles` for all children linked to this parent
  - Pass to client component
- Create src/app/parent/ai-conversations/ai-conversations-client.tsx:
  - Learner selector dropdown
  - Paginated conversation history (grouped by date)
  - Safety flag indicators (red badge on flagged messages using `metadata.flagged`)
- Add nav item to src/app/parent/layout.tsx under the Family group: `{ href: "/parent/ai-conversations", label: "AI Conversations", icon: "💬" }`

**Step 2.5 — Safety alert emails to parents**
- Create src/lib/email/safety-alert-email.ts using the existing Resend pattern from src/lib/email/parent-consent-email.ts:
  - `sendSafetyFlagEmail(params: { parentEmail, childName, flaggedMessage, categories, timestamp }): Promise<EmailResult>`
- Call from the moderation layer in step 2.1 when `moderateInput()` or `moderateOutput()` returns `flagged: true`
- Look up the parent's email via `parent_consents` table using the child's `user_id`

**Step 2.6 — AI subscription-tier gating**
- In src/app/api/companion/chat/route.ts, add daily interaction cap for free users (e.g., 5/day) using the same pattern as `AI_TUTOR_DAILY_LIMIT` in the tutor route
- In src/app/api/ai/tutor/route.ts, differentiate limits: free tier gets 3 questions/day, premium gets 25/day (read from `app_settings` or env vars)
- Gate src/lib/ai/follow-up.ts and src/lib/ai/remediation-worksheet.ts behind `checkPremiumAccessServer()` — free users get the rule-based fallback only, never the OpenAI path
- In dashboard components (ai-tutor-panel.tsx, ai-coach-card.tsx), show an upgrade prompt when the daily limit is reached instead of disabling the UI

**Step 2.7 — Server-side lesson gate**
- In [src/app/lessons/\[lessonId\]/page.tsx](src/app/lessons/[lessonId]/page.tsx), after resolving the lesson:
  - Call `isModuleFree(moduleId)` from src/lib/content-gating
  - If not free, call `checkPremiumAccessServer(supabase, userId)` from src/lib/billing/entitlement-check.ts
  - If `!result.active`, redirect to a new `/upgrade` page showing the paywall
  - Unauthenticated users hitting premium lessons redirect to `/auth/sign-in` with a `?next=` return URL

**Step 2.8 — Multi-learner profile cap**
- In src/app/student/onboarding/page.tsx, before the profile creation API call:
  - Fetch existing learner count via `GET /api/student/profiles/count` (or query `student_profiles` count in the page's server component)
  - If free-tier and count >= 1 (configurable), show an upgrade prompt instead of the onboarding form
- Also gate the "Add Learner" button on the parent dashboard with the same check

**Step 2.9 — Upgrade nudges and pricing page**
- Create src/app/pricing/page.tsx — a comparison table: Free (daily limit, 1 learner, rule-based AI, basic reporting) vs Premium (unlimited, multi-learner, full AI, detailed reporting, offline mode)
- Add a non-intrusive upgrade card at lesson completion in the lesson experience component — shown only to free-tier users after completing a lesson (natural conversion moment)
- Fix src/app/explore/_components/upgrade-banner.tsx to call `isPremiumUser()` and hide for premium subscribers

---

### Phase 3 — Parent Trust, B2B, and Certification (Medium Priority)

**Step 3.1 — Curriculum progress reports for parents**
- Extend src/app/parent/reports/parent-reports-client.tsx to show:
  - Lessons completed count from `user_learning_progress` (count rows per `user_id`)
  - Average mastery from `user_skill_mastery` (avg of `mastery_level`)
  - Accuracy % from `user_skill_mastery` (`correct_attempts / attempts`)
  - Next review dates from `user_learning_progress.next_review_at`
  - Subject breakdown (group by skill/module categories)
- Add the required Supabase queries in src/app/parent/reports/page.tsx alongside the existing language data queries

**Step 3.2 — PIN / cognitive gate on parent portal**
- Create src/lib/parent-gate.ts with `generateArithmeticChallenge(): { question: string, answer: number }` — randomized text-form arithmetic (e.g., "What is fourteen multiplied by seven?") per Apple/Google kids app guidelines
- Create a `<ParentGate />` client component that intercepts entry to the parent portal — shown as a modal before rendering child routes
- Store gate pass in `sessionStorage` (expires on tab close)
- Wire into src/app/parent/layout.tsx as a wrapper around the `DashShell` children

**Step 3.3 — Parent-facing DSAR UI**
- Add a "Your Data Rights" section to the parent compliance page (or as a sibling route src/app/parent/data-rights/page.tsx):
  - Form to submit access/erasure/portability requests (reuse the existing `POST /api/privacy/dsar` endpoint with a parent wrapper)
  - Status tracker showing all submitted requests
  - Self-service data export download when request is completed

**Step 3.4 — Activate school licensing SKU**
- In src/lib/language-learning/pricing-spec.ts, change `language_school_annual.status` from `"planned"` to `"active"`
- Wire the SKU into the Stripe product catalog and the entitlement check flow

**Step 3.5 — Educator landing page**
- Create src/app/for-schools/page.tsx — marketing page explaining:
  - Free classroom access for teachers
  - Learner engagement analytics
  - How home access drives family subscriptions (the Epic! model)
  - CTA: "Set Up Your Classroom" → educator registration flow
- The organizations infrastructure already exists in src/lib/organizations/ (roles, membership, invoicing, roster, reporting, tokens) — the landing page just needs to connect to it

**Step 3.6 — Educator onboarding flow**
- Create src/app/for-schools/register/page.tsx:
  - School name, teacher name, email, class size
  - Creates an organization via the existing org infrastructure
  - Generates class codes via src/lib/organizations/tokens.ts (`buildProvisioningToken`)
  - Sets `user_profiles.is_teacher = true`

**Step 3.7 — Privacy certification preparation**
- Create src/app/components/ui/privacy-seals.tsx — a reusable component that renders kidSAFE and Common Sense Privacy seal badges (placeholder images until certifications are obtained)
- Add seal badges to the home page footer, pricing page, and parent portal header
- Create docs/common-sense-self-assessment.md — internal document tracking scores against the 200-point Common Sense Privacy rubric
- Begin kidSAFE application once Phase 1 steps (1.1–1.7) are complete

**Step 3.8 — Report gating for parent portal**
- In src/app/parent/reports/page.tsx, check `checkPremiumAccessServer()`:
  - Free tier sees a summary preview (learner count, last activity date) with an upgrade CTA
  - Premium tier sees full detailed reporting

---

### Verification

**Per phase:**
- **Phase 1:** `npx tsc --noEmit --skipLibCheck` passes; manual test: create a child account → verify analytics are suppressed; visit `/legal/children-privacy`; test consent revocation flow; verify data retention cron deletes expired records
- **Phase 2:** Test AI moderation by sending flagged content to companion/tutor endpoints → verify safe fallback returned and event logged; hit `/lessons/[premiumLessonId]` as free user → verify redirect to upgrade page; create 2nd learner profile as free user → verify cap enforced
- **Phase 3:** Access parent portal → verify PIN gate appears; visit `/for-schools` → verify landing page renders; check parent reports show curriculum data

**Automated:**
- Add integration tests for moderation layer (mock OpenAI Moderation API responses)
- Add test for lesson gate redirect logic
- Add test for data retention cron (mock Supabase delete calls)

---

### Decisions

- **Daily activity cap number:** 10 activities/day (matching ABCmouse Basic) — configurable via `app_settings`
- **Free learner cap:** 1 profile per free account (matching report recommendation for multi-child gating)
- **AI daily limits:** Free: 5 companion + 3 tutor/day; Premium: 50 companion + 25 tutor/day
- **Data retention window:** 12 months active, 90 days post-deletion, 7 days for pending consent rows
- **Moderation API:** OpenAI Moderation API (free, already have OpenAI key) rather than a third-party service
- **Parent gate mechanism:** Randomized text-form arithmetic (Apple/Google compliant) rather than PIN (easier for parents, harder for children)
- **Email transport:** Resend API (already in use for 3 email types) — no new dependencies
- **Phase 1 files created/modified:** ~12 files; **Phase 2:** ~15 files; **Phase 3:** ~12 files

User: Daily activity cap number: 20 activities/day (matching ABCmouse Basic) — configurable via app_settings= make adjustable in the owner console for promotions or per user. 
Free learner cap: 1 profile per free account (matching report recommendation for multi-child gating)=yes make adjustable in owner console
AI daily limits: Free: 5 companion + 3 tutor/day; Premium: 50 companion + 25 tutor/day =yes make adjustable in owner console
Data retention window: 12 months active, 90 days post-deletion, 7 days for pending consent rows-yes
Moderation API: OpenAI Moderation API (free, already have OpenAI key) rather than a third-party service=yes but whichever moderation LLM is more precise -we can reserch later and swith just make that switchable in the owner console, likely just API key swap
Parent gate mechanism: Randomized text-form arithmetic (Apple/Google compliant) rather than PIN (easier for parents, harder for children)=yes
Email transport: Resend API (already in use for 3 email types) — no new dependencies=yes

I have all the context needed. Here's the comprehensive implementation plan:

---

## Plan: Configurable Limits, Gates & Safety Infrastructure

The `app_settings` table already exists in the DB schema but has zero application code. This plan builds a full **runtime-configurable settings system** in the admin console, then wires it into 7 enforcement points: daily activity caps, learner profile limits, AI daily limits (free/premium split), content moderation (provider-abstracted), parent gate, data retention, and email alerts. All numeric limits become admin-adjustable at runtime without redeployment.

**Steps**

### Phase 0: App Settings Infrastructure

1. **Create `src/lib/settings/app-settings.ts`** — server-side helper that reads from the `app_settings` table via admin client. Exports `getAppSetting<T>(key: string, fallback: T): Promise<T>` and `setAppSetting(key: string, value: unknown, userId: string): Promise<void>`. Cache settings in-memory with a 60-second TTL to avoid per-request DB hits. Define a `SETTINGS_KEYS` const object with all known keys and their defaults:
   - `daily_activity_cap` → `20`
   - `free_learner_cap` → `1`
   - `ai_companion_limit_free` → `5`
   - `ai_companion_limit_premium` → `50`
   - `ai_tutor_limit_free` → `3`
   - `ai_tutor_limit_premium` → `25`
   - `data_retention_active_months` → `12`
   - `data_retention_post_deletion_days` → `90`
   - `data_retention_pending_consent_days` → `7`
   - `moderation_provider` → `"openai"` (future: swap to another provider)
   - `moderation_enabled` → `true`

2. **Create `src/lib/settings/per-user-overrides.ts`** — for per-user limit overrides (promotions). Uses a new `user_setting_overrides` table (or a JSONB column on `user_profiles`). Exports `getUserOverride(userId: string, key: string): Promise<number | null>`. When set, overrides the global `app_settings` value for that user. Admin can set these from the console.

3. **Add RLS policies for `app_settings`** — add a migration SQL creating two policies: admin-read (`SELECT` where `is_admin = true` via joined `user_profiles`) and admin-write (`INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE` where `is_admin = true`). Alternatively, since the table already has RLS enabled with no policies, continue using service-role client only (no policy needed — the `requireAdminForApi()` guard + admin client handles access).

4. **Create `src/app/api/admin/settings/route.ts`** — `GET` returns all settings (keyed map), `POST` accepts `{ key, value }` and upserts. Both gated by `requireAdminForApi()`. Also a `GET /api/admin/settings/[key]` route for individual reads.

5. **Create `src/app/api/admin/settings/user-overrides/route.ts`** — `GET ?userId=...` returns per-user overrides, `POST { userId, key, value }` upserts, `DELETE { userId, key }` removes.

6. **Create `src/app/admin/settings/page.tsx`** + **`settings-client.tsx`** — new admin console page. UI has sections matching the `SETTINGS_KEYS` categories:
   - **Activity Limits** — daily activity cap slider/input
   - **Learner Profiles** — free learner cap slider/input
   - **AI Limits** — four inputs (companion free/premium, tutor free/premium)
   - **Data Retention** — three inputs (active months, post-deletion days, pending consent days)
   - **Content Moderation** — toggle (enabled/disabled) + provider dropdown (openai / future providers)
   - **Per-User Overrides** — search-by-email, display user, set individual limit overrides
   - Each field shows current value, updated_by, updated_at. Uses the same pure-React + fetch pattern as operations-console.tsx.

7. **Add "Settings" to admin sidebar** — add a new `DashNavGroup` item in src/app/admin/layout.tsx, inside the "Operations" group: `{ href: "/admin/settings", label: "Settings", icon: "⚙" }` (or use a different icon to distinguish from Console, e.g., `"⛭"` or `"🔧"`).

8. **Seed default settings** — add a migration or seed script that inserts default values for all `SETTINGS_KEYS` into `app_settings` if they don't exist.

---

### Phase 1: Daily Activity Cap (20/day default)

9. **Create `src/lib/limits/daily-activity.ts`** — exports `getDailyActivityUsage(supabase, userId): Promise<{ used: number, limit: number, remaining: number, limitReached: boolean }>`. Counts rows in `learning_events` where `user_id = userId` and `event_type = 'activity_interacted'` and `created_at >= start of UTC day`. Reads limit from `getAppSetting('daily_activity_cap', 20)`, then checks `getUserOverride(userId, 'daily_activity_cap')`.

10. **Enforce in lesson page** — modify [src/app/lessons/[lessonId]/page.tsx](src/app/lessons/[lessonId]/page.tsx) to call `getDailyActivityUsage()`. If `limitReached` and user is NOT premium, show a "daily limit reached" interstitial with upgrade CTA instead of lesson content. Premium users bypass this cap (or use their own higher per-user override).

11. **Enforce on activity submission** — find the API route or client function that inserts `learning_events` with `event_type = 'activity_interacted'`. Add a server-side check that rejects submissions when the daily cap is hit for free users.

---

### Phase 2: Free Learner Cap (1 profile default)

12. **Create `src/lib/limits/learner-cap.ts`** — exports `canCreateLearnerProfile(supabase, accountId): Promise<{ allowed: boolean, currentCount: number, limit: number }>`. Counts rows in `student_profiles` where `account_id = accountId`. Reads limit from `getAppSetting('free_learner_cap', 1)` + per-user override. Premium users get unlimited (or a much higher cap like 10).

13. **Enforce in onboarding** — modify src/app/student/onboarding/page.tsx at the start (before Step 1). Call `canCreateLearnerProfile()`. If `!allowed`, redirect to an upgrade page or show an inline "upgrade to add more learners" message.

14. **Enforce in placement diagnostic API** — modify src/app/api/ai/placement-diagnostic/route.ts (the `POST` handler around line 508-522 where `createProfile()` is called). Add server-side count check before inserting. Return 403 with `{ error: "learner_limit_reached" }`.

---

### Phase 3: AI Daily Limits (Free/Premium Split)

15. **Refactor AI tutor limit** — modify src/app/api/ai/tutor/route.ts `getTutorDailyLimit()` at line 328. Instead of reading `serverEnv.AI_TUTOR_DAILY_LIMIT`, call `getAppSetting()` with key `ai_tutor_limit_free` (3) or `ai_tutor_limit_premium` (25) based on the user's subscription status via `checkPremiumAccessServer()`. Then check per-user override. Keep the env var as an ultimate fallback.

16. **Add companion chat limits** — modify src/app/api/companion/chat/route.ts:
    - Add auth: call `createSupabaseServerClient().auth.getUser()`. If no user, return 401.
    - Add daily limit check: count rows in `ai_tutor_conversations` (or a new tracking mechanism) where source identifies companion messages for today. Use `getAppSetting('ai_companion_limit_free', 5)` vs `ai_companion_limit_premium` (50).
    - Add DB logging: insert conversation rows into `ai_tutor_conversations` with a distinguishing `metadata.source = 'companion'` or add a `channel` column.
    - Add rate limiting: call `enforceIpRateLimit()` from src/lib/security/ip-rate-limit.ts.

17. **Create `src/lib/limits/ai-limits.ts`** — shared helper for both tutor and companion: `getAiDailyUsage(supabase, userId, channel: 'tutor' | 'companion'): Promise<AiUsage>`. Reads the appropriate settings key, checks subscription tier, checks per-user override.

---

### Phase 4: Content Moderation (Provider-Abstracted)

18. **Create `src/lib/ai/moderation.ts`** — defines a `ModerationProvider` interface: `{ checkContent(text: string): Promise<ModerationResult> }` where `ModerationResult = { flagged: boolean, categories: Record<string, boolean>, scores: Record<string, number> }`. Implement `OpenAIModerationProvider` using the OpenAI Moderation API (free endpoint, uses existing `OPENAI_API_KEY`). Export `getModeration(): ModerationProvider` which reads `getAppSetting('moderation_provider', 'openai')` and returns the matching provider. Export `moderateContent(text: string): Promise<ModerationResult>` as the main API.

19. **Wire moderation into companion chat** — in src/app/api/companion/chat/route.ts, call `moderateContent(message)` on the user's input before sending to OpenAI. If flagged, return a safe generic response ("I'm not sure about that. Let's talk about something from your lessons!"). Also moderate the AI response before returning.

20. **Wire moderation into AI tutor** — in src/app/api/ai/tutor/route.ts, add `moderateContent()` calls on input and output. If input flagged → return educational redirect. If output flagged → return rule-based fallback.

21. **Admin toggle** — the `moderation_enabled` setting in the Settings page controls whether moderation runs. When disabled, `moderateContent()` returns `{ flagged: false }` immediately.

---

### Phase 5: Parent Gate (Arithmetic Challenge)

22. **Create `src/lib/parent-gate.ts`** — exports `generateParentGateChallenge(): { question: string, answer: number, token: string }` and `verifyParentGateAnswer(token: string, answer: number): boolean`. Generates randomized arithmetic (e.g., "What is 47 + 28?" or "What is 156 - 89?") — numbers large enough that children can't easily solve but simple for adults. The `token` is an HMAC-signed payload containing the answer and a 5-minute expiry, using the existing `PARENT_CONSENT_TOKEN_SECRET` from src/lib/compliance/parent-consent-token.ts.

23. **Create `src/components/parent-gate-modal.tsx`** — a modal/dialog component with the arithmetic question in **text form** (not an image — Apple/Google compliant). Renders the question, a number input, submit button, and "Try another question" link. On correct answer, stores a session-scoped flag (sessionStorage or short-lived cookie) so the parent isn't re-challenged during the same session.

24. **Wire into parent portal** — add the parent gate check at the top of src/app/parent/ layout or individual pages. If the session flag is absent, show the `ParentGateModal` before rendering the parent dashboard.

---

### Phase 6: Data Retention

25. **Create `src/app/api/admin/data-retention/route.ts`** — `POST` endpoint (protected by `requireAdminForApi()` or a cron secret). Reads retention windows from `app_settings`:
    - Delete `learning_events` older than `data_retention_active_months` (12)
    - Delete soft-deleted user data older than `data_retention_post_deletion_days` (90)
    - Delete unverified `parent_consents` rows where `status = 'pending'` and `requested_at` older than `data_retention_pending_consent_days` (7)
    - Logs actions to `admin_action_logs`

26. **Add retention trigger to admin console** — add a "Run Data Retention" button in the Settings page or Compliance page, calling the above endpoint. Also document how to wire this to a cron (Vercel Cron, Supabase pg_cron, etc.).

---

### Phase 7: Safety Alert Emails

27. **Create `src/lib/email/safety-alert-email.ts`** — exports `sendSafetyAlertEmail({ parentEmail, childName, flaggedContent, category, timestamp })`. Uses the existing Resend infrastructure from src/lib/email/parent-consent-email.ts. Sends when moderation flags content in a child's AI conversation.

28. **Wire into moderation flow** — when `moderateContent()` flags input or output in companion/tutor routes, look up the child's parent email from `parent_consents` or `user_profiles`, then call `sendSafetyAlertEmail()`. Make this async (don't block the response).

---

### Phase 8: Schema Migration

29. **Create migration file** — SQL to:
    - Add `user_setting_overrides` table: `(user_id UUID references auth.users, key TEXT, value JSONB, updated_by UUID, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ, PRIMARY KEY(user_id, key))`
    - Add RLS policies for `user_setting_overrides` (admin-only)
    - Seed `app_settings` with all default values
    - Optionally add `channel` column to `ai_tutor_conversations` to distinguish companion vs tutor messages (or use metadata JSONB)

---

**Verification**

1. **Settings page**: Navigate to `/admin/settings`, verify all settings display with defaults. Change a value, verify it persists across page reload. Set a per-user override, verify it takes effect.
2. **Daily activity cap**: Log in as free user, complete 20 activities, verify 21st is blocked. Change cap to 25 in admin settings, verify the change takes effect within 60 seconds (cache TTL).
3. **Learner cap**: As free user, create 1 profile, attempt to create a 2nd — verify blocked. Upgrade to premium, verify 2nd profile allowed.
4. **AI limits**: As free user, send 5 companion messages — verify 6th is rejected with friendly message. Send 3 tutor questions — verify 4th is rejected. As premium, verify higher limits.
5. **Moderation**: Send a message containing flagged content to companion/tutor. Verify safe fallback response returned, parent email sent, conversation logged.
6. **Parent gate**: Navigate to parent portal — verify arithmetic modal appears. Enter wrong answer — verify rejection. Enter correct answer — verify access granted for session.
7. **Data retention**: Click "Run Data Retention" in admin. Verify old `learning_events` deleted, pending consents cleaned up.
8. **Per-user overrides**: In admin settings, set a specific user's `daily_activity_cap` to 50. Verify that user gets 50 while others still get 20.

**Decisions**
- **`app_settings` access pattern**: Use admin (service-role) client only — avoids needing RLS policies; `requireAdminForApi()` provides the auth gate
- **Companion chat tracking**: Reuse `ai_tutor_conversations` table with a `metadata.channel = 'companion'` discriminator rather than creating a new table — reduces migration complexity
- **Per-user overrides**: Separate `user_setting_overrides` table rather than JSONB column on `user_profiles` — cleaner querying, avoids profile schema bloat
- **Moderation provider abstraction**: Interface-based with runtime provider selection via `app_settings` — future providers only need to implement `ModerationProvider` interface
- **Parent gate crypto**: Reuse existing `PARENT_CONSENT_TOKEN_SECRET` for HMAC signing — no new secrets needed
- **Cache TTL**: 60 seconds for settings cache — balances responsiveness with DB load. Admin UI can include a "flush cache" button for immediate effect

User: app_settings access pattern: Use admin (service-role) client only — avoids needing RLS policies; requireAdminForApi() provides the auth gate=yes
Companion chat tracking: Reuse ai_tutor_conversations table with a metadata.channel = 'companion' discriminator rather than creating a new table — reduces migration complexity=yes
Per-user overrides: Separate user_setting_overrides table rather than JSONB column on user_profiles — cleaner querying, avoids profile schema bloat=yes
Moderation provider abstraction: Interface-based with runtime provider selection via app_settings — future providers only need to implement ModerationProvider interface=yes
Parent gate crypto: Reuse existing PARENT_CONSENT_TOKEN_SECRET for HMAC signing — no new secrets needed=yes
Cache TTL: 60 seconds for settings cache — balances responsiveness with DB load. Admin UI can include a "flush cache" button for immediate effect=yes
